title: Emmett Till Antilynching Act
summary: Would have established a specific federal criminal civil-rights violation for lynching, reviving a reform Congress had failed to enact for more than a century.
outcome_summary: The House passed H.R. 35 in February 2020, but the bill stalled in the Senate, extending Congress's long record of delayed federal action against racial-terror violence.
impact_notes: Important because even a narrowly framed anti-lynching measure still failed to become law at that stage, despite overwhelming historical evidence of federal inaction.

scores.notes: A major symbolic and legal reform that remained blocked despite the long history of failed anti-lynching legislation.

sources.notes: Official bill page showing House passage and Senate receipt.

title: Justice in Policing Act of 2020
summary: Would have imposed federal policing standards and expanded accountability measures on issues including use of force, chokeholds, no-knock warrants, data collection, and officer misconduct.
outcome_summary: The House passed H.R. 7120 in June 2020 after nationwide protests over police violence, but the Senate did not enact the measure, leaving major federal policing reforms unrealized.
impact_notes: A major failed accountability package that prefigured the later George Floyd Justice in Policing Act and reflected how difficult it remained to pass federal policing reform.

scores.notes: One of the highest-profile blocked policing reforms of the 2020 protest era.

sources.source_title: H.R.7120 - George Floyd Justice in Policing Act of 2020
sources.notes: Official action history showing House passage and subsequent Senate placement on the legislative calendar.

sources.source_title: S.3912 - Justice in Policing Act of 2020
sources.notes: Official Senate companion bill page summarizing the proposed reforms.

title: CROWN Act of 2022
summary: Would have prohibited discrimination based on hair texture or hairstyles commonly associated with race across employment, housing, public accommodations, and federally assisted programs.
outcome_summary: The House passed the CROWN Act in March 2022, but the bill stalled in the Senate, leaving a visible gap in federal civil-rights protection against race-linked hair discrimination.
impact_notes: Its failure mattered because it targeted a well-documented form of race discrimination that often falls on Black students, workers, and tenants.

scores.notes: A blocked anti-discrimination reform aimed at a specific but widespread racialized practice.

sources.notes: Official bill page showing House passage and Senate referral.
CRS summary describing the bill's anti-discrimination provisions.

title: Justice for Black Farmers Act of 2023
summary: Would have addressed USDA discrimination against Black farmers through debt relief, land-retention and land-grant support, civil-rights reforms, and equity-focused agricultural investment.
outcome_summary: The bill directly confronted the documented history of federal discrimination against Black farmers, but it remained stuck in committee and did not produce the structural remedies it proposed.
impact_notes: A major blocked reparative and anti-discrimination agriculture measure because it named the problem directly and proposed institutional remedies that Congress did not advance.

scores.notes: A high-value blocked reform aimed at one of the clearest long-run sites of federal racial discrimination.

sources.source_title: H.R.1167 - Justice for Black Farmers Act of 2023
sources.notes: Official House bill page and CRS summary explaining the proposed remedies for discrimination against Black farmers.

sources.source_title: S.96 - Justice for Black Farmers Act of 2023
sources.notes: Senate companion history showing the measure's referral and lack of further progress.
